name: CASULO Capability-State Ontology
pt_name: Ontologia de Capacidades e Estados CASULO
purpose: Organize operations by computable capabilities, states, evidence, gates, automation and human intervention rather than traditional departments.

principles.name: Capability before department
principles.description: Classify what must be done before classifying where it sits in an org chart.

principles.name: State before solution
principles.description: No solution should be created before the operational state is mapped.

principles.name: Evidence before claim
principles.description: No claim is valid without evidence state.

principles.name: Gate before action
principles.description: No execution, merge, production activation or external claim without gate clearance.

principles.name: Automation where safe
principles.description: Automate repeatable and governable work, keep humans for judgment, design and review.

principles.name: Response economy
principles.description: Responses must be precise, economical, state-grounded and action-oriented.

layers.name: Operating Principles
layers.description: Global rules for safety, economy, evidence and state-based decisions.

layers.name: Universal Capabilities
layers.description: Core actions any domain can require.

layers.name: Operational States
layers.description: Canonical state classes used before any output or action.

layers.name: Evidence and Gates
layers.description: Controls that decide what can be claimed or executed.

layers.name: Automation and Human Intervention
layers.description: Defines what can be automated and where human review is mandatory.

layers.name: Delivery Modes
layers.description: Maps state into diagnosis, cockpit, solution, operation, training or monitoring.

layers.name: Response Governor
layers.description: Controls answer style, precision, boundaries and next allowed action.
